page: Google Cloud Platform c2-standard-30 is a compute-optimized server instance featuring 30 vCPUs mapped to 15 physical cores of an Intel Xeon processor running at 3.1 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, utilizing hyperthreading with 2.0 threads per core. Memory capacity is 120.0 GB, providing a ratio of 4.0 GB per core. The CPU cache configuration includes 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 24 MB L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the average tier for most compute tasks. The stress-ng single-core score is 1561.19, and the multi-core score is 30787.06. Memory performance is a notable strength, with a PassMark memory score of 2815.71, categorizing it in the top 25% tier. Large-block uncached memory read bandwidth reaches 113.91 GB/sec, while write bandwidth is 48.85 GB/sec. In application-specific tests, the instance achieves a static web serving throughput of 18.82 GB/sec and a Redis SET performance of 27.0 million operations per second.

Tradeoffs are evident in large-scale workloads. While the instance handles small and medium LLM inference at average speeds, prompt processing for large 70B models drops to a poor 4.37 tokens per second, and text generation data is unavailable due to resource constraints. The lack of local storage requires external block storage attachment. This instance is suited for CPU-bound workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, image processing, and database operations.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2-standard-30 is a compute-optimized server instance configured with 30 vCPUs and 120.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 3.1 GHz across 15 physical cores, it features dedicated CPU allocation and hyperthreading. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data shows average performance across most metrics, including a stress-ng multi-core score of 30787.06 and a static web serving throughput of 18.82 GB/sec. However, memory performance is strong, ranking in the top 25% with a PassMark memory score of 2815.71. Tradeoffs include poor performance on large LLM inference tasks, with prompt processing for a 70B model limited to 4.37 tokens per second. This server is designed for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, database operations, and medium-scale web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2-standard-30 is a compute-optimized server with 30 vCPUs, 120 GB RAM, and a 3.1 GHz Intel Xeon CPU. It delivers strong memory performance but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2-standard-30 is a compute-optimized server featuring 30 vCPUs, 120 GB RAM, and a 3.1 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and strong memory performance for CPU-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 30 dedicated vCPUs on a 3.1 GHz Intel Xeon processor
120.0 GB of RAM with strong top-tier memory benchmark scores
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Average performance for web serving and database operations
Poor performance on large-scale 70B LLM inference workloads
